name: La Hoya de Buñol - Circular from Montroi
name_original: La Hoya de Buñol — Circular desde Montroi
region_intro: Mountain biking around Montroi / Montroy offers diverse terrain within the Alcalans Valley, characterized by rolling hills, fruit tree orchards, and the Río Magro valley. The region features a mix of surfaces, from asphalt and uphill tracks to more technical downhill trails. This landscape provides varied mountain bike trails suitable for different skill levels, including sections with good flow and others with challenging climbs. The area's natural features include scenic viewpoints and varied landscapes such as forests and rocky terrain. Best mountain bike trails around Montroi / Montroy The most popular mountain bike trail is La Hoya de Buñol - Circular from Montroi, a 30.1 miles (48.4 km) trail that takes 4 hours 14 minutes to complete, offering varied terrain and scenic views. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Tres Pins Recreation Area – View of Dos Aguas loop from Montroi, a difficult 45.1 miles (72.6 km) path featuring significant elevation gain and expansive vistas. Local mountain bikers also love the Masía de Pavía – Cortitxelles Trails loop from Montroi, a 24.0 miles (38.7 km) trail leading through rolling hills and natural landscapes, often completed in about 2 hours 49 minutes. Mountain biking around Montroi / Montroy is defined by rolling hills, fruit tree orchards, and the Río Magro valley. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate half-day outings to more challenging routes. The routes in Montroi / Montroy are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.2 stars from around 10 reviews. More than 190 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Montroi / Montroy's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are available around Montroi / Montroy?
A: There are over 30 mountain bike trails around Montroi / Montroy, offering a diverse range of options for different skill levels. The komoot community has explored these routes over 190 times.

Q: What is the general difficulty level of mountain biking in Montroi / Montroy?
A: The trails in Montroi / Montroy offer a mix of difficulties. You'll find 9 easy routes, 17 moderate routes, and 4 difficult routes. While some sections can be technical, many trails are enjoyable for a half-day outing with good flow, while others feature challenging climbs.

Q: Are there any easy mountain bike trails suitable for beginners in Montroi / Montroy?
A: Yes, there are 9 easy mountain bike routes available in the area, perfect for beginners or those looking for a more relaxed ride. These trails often wind through rolling hills and fruit tree orchards, providing picturesque scenery without overly technical demands.

Q: Are there challenging mountain bike trails for experienced riders?
A: Absolutely. For experienced riders seeking a challenge, there are 4 difficult routes. One such route is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14694870" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tres Pins Recreation Area – View of Dos Aguas loop from Montroi&lt;/a&gt;, which is 45.1 miles (72.6 km) long and features significant elevation gain and expansive vistas.

Q: What natural features or scenic viewpoints can I expect to see along the mountain bike trails?
A: The region is rich in natural beauty. You can encounter the picturesque &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4058748" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Barranc de Galleç Waterfall&lt;/a&gt;, or enjoy quiet relaxation and beautiful views of the Río Magro valley from &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2593714" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;L'Estepar Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt;. Many trails also offer views of rolling hills, fruit tree orchards, and the Río Magro itself.

Q: What cultural or historical sites can I explore near the mountain bike routes?
A: Beyond the trails, Montroy offers historical points of interest such as a 16th-century church and a Moorish tower dating back to the 13th century. The Valencian Museum of Honey also highlights the region's deep-rooted beekeeping tradition.

Q: Are there any circular mountain bike routes in Montroi / Montroy?
A: Yes, many routes in the area are circular, allowing you to start and end in the same location. An example is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e910612490" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Hoya de Buñol - Circular from Montroi&lt;/a&gt;, a 30.1-mile (48.4 km) trail offering varied terrain and scenic views.

Q: What is the best time of year for mountain biking in Montroi / Montroy?
A: Montroy's mild climate, with nearly 2,700 hours of sunshine annually, makes it appealing for outdoor activities throughout the year. Hot summers and mild winters mean you can enjoy the trails in most seasons, though spring and autumn offer particularly pleasant temperatures.

Q: Are there any family-friendly mountain bike trails in Montroi / Montroy?
A: While specific 'family-friendly' designations are not always explicit, the 9 easy routes in the area are generally suitable for families with some biking experience. These trails typically feature less challenging terrain and elevation.

Q: What do other mountain bikers enjoy about the trails in Montroi / Montroy?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.2 stars from around 10 reviews. Riders often praise the diverse terrain, scenic views of rolling hills and orchards, and the mix of challenging climbs and flowing sections.

Q: Are there mountain bike trails that pass by historical landmarks?
A: Yes, some routes incorporate historical elements. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/39554947" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Viewpoint of the Torre de Montroy – Tower of the Castle of Montroy loop from Montroi&lt;/a&gt; takes you past significant local landmarks, offering both a ride and a glimpse into the region's past.

Q: Is there parking available near the mountain bike trailheads in Montroi / Montroy?
A: Montroi / Montroy is generally accessible, and parking is typically available in or near the town center, which serves as a common starting point for many routes. Specific parking details for individual trailheads may vary, but the area is well-suited for visitors arriving by car.
tips: El Xarxanet viewpoint with amazing views of the Hoya de Buñol and its surroundings. It is reached by bike along a well-firmed path.
Stretch of road in excellent condition with incredible views of the Hoya de Buñol and the Sierra del Ave. Ideal for riding a mtb or gravel bike and enjoying the beautiful landscapes!
Site surrounded by nature in a very quiet and relaxing environment with very nice views of the Rambla de Altury. You get there by a trail for mtb with several technical and quite narrow sectors.
